what_it_does: Defines a reusable JSON Schema object shape for human-readable legibility fields: a required human name ('name') and plain-language kind ('is'), plus optional 'fills' and 'why', intended to be carried by any addressable type so non-developer surfaces can describe what a thing is without relying on machine names or glyphs.
observations: Uses JSON Schema draft 2020-12 with a custom $id URI scheme 'vi-vision://global/schemas/v1/legibility.schema.json'
Title and description frame the schema as 'meaning-fields' for a non-developer reader named Tim
Requires exactly 'name' and 'is'; 'fills' and 'why' are optional
Sets additionalProperties: false, locking the field-set to four named keys
Description embeds an 'operator-legibility law' with rules for naming, rendering, and per-instance name resolution
Mentions runtime/legibility.js and decision.schema.json as related consumers/contracts
standouts: The 'name' + 'is' pairing is treated as a hard, required human-voice contract
Plain meaning-words must lead and glyphs must only decorate
Per-instance name resolution is specified: declared name -> derived human name -> raw leaf as floor
additionalProperties: false makes the field-set strictly closed
concerns: The schema description encodes procedural/UI rules (render order, humanisation algorithm, last-resort floor) that JSON Schema cannot validate
The $id uses a non-HTTP custom URI scheme with no visible resolution mechanism
Cross-file relationships are only mentioned narratively; there are no $ref links in the schema body
notes: This file is the schema contract for the human-readable 'name + is' label pair that surfaces are expected to render for any addressable thing.
questions: What is the exact algorithm for 'humanise the leaf' when deriving a name?
How does validateLegibility enforce the operator-legibility law beyond schema validation?
Are 'fills' and 'why' meant to be single sentences, full paragraphs, or constrained in length?
Is this schema referenced via $ref from decision.schema.json, or only described as related?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and content align: it declares a reusable, required name/is field-set with optional enrichment and a closed shape.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to guarantee that every addressable item in the system can be introduced to a non-developer in the same voice, no matter which UI surface displays it.
novelty: Embedding an explicit 'operator-legibility law' and a per-instance name-resolution fallback directly into a JSON Schema's description, treating human-readable naming as a first-class contract.
contribution: A portable, enforceable field-set contract for human-readable labels that other schemas can adopt to keep surfaces consistent.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON Schema defining the required 'name' and 'is' human-readable legibility fields plus optional 'fills' and 'why', with additionalProperties false, for attaching plain-language identity to addressable types.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reusable schema contract so every addressable thing exposes a consistent human name and kind for non-developer surfaces.
